+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-106
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-23_09-49-07+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-106_EXEC_2026-05-23_09-49-07+0300.md — this report
+STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-106_TASK_2026-05-23_09-40-59+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. This was a read-only egui host design preflight. No production code,
+  tests, manifests, workflows, scripts, lints, baselines, change.md, or
+  existing packets were modified.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None.
+
+### Docs
+
+- None.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Only one new file was added: this EXEC report. No other files were created,
+edited, deleted, or renamed.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+This was a read-only egui host design preflight. **No local cargo commands,
+tests, formatters, architecture lints, or `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` were
+run.** All "results" below are read-only inspection outcomes only.
+
+### Pre-audit working-tree check
+
+```
+git status --short --untracked-files=no
+```
+→ empty output. No pre-existing tracked changes to the audit surfaces. The
+working tree was clean of tracked modifications before the audit began.
+Read-only conclusions are therefore not contaminated by stale local edits.
+
+### Audit-root enumeration
+
+Used `Glob` (the harness's recursive file-listing tool) to enumerate each
+mandatory audit root; each enumeration returned a non-empty file list,
+confirming every required root exists and is readable:
+
+- `editor/rge-editor/**/*` — present (3 files: `Cargo.toml`,
+  `src/main.rs`, `src/glb_watcher.rs`).
+- `crates/editor-shell/**/*` — present (35 files: `src/` + `tests/`).
+- `crates/editor-ui/**/*` — present (45 files: `src/` + `tests/` +
+  `assets/defaults/*.ron`).
+- `crates/editor-state/**/*` — present (11 files: `src/` + `tests/`).
+- `crates/editor-actions/**/*` — present (13 files: `src/` + `tests/`).
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/**/*` — present (9 files including
+  `Cargo.toml`, `src/lib.rs`, `src/handoff.rs`, `src/tabs.rs`, and 5
+  test files). **Per the TASK packet, only
+  `crates/editor-egui-host/Cargo.toml` was opened. The `src/**` and
+  `tests/**` paths under this crate were not read.**
+
+### Text searches across the mandatory audit roots
+
+Used `Grep` (the harness's ripgrep front-end) against each audit root for
+the required search terms (`egui`, `egui_winit`, `egui_wgpu`, `Egui`,
+`DockState`, `snapshot`, `Inspector`, `RenderInput`, `render_frame`,
+`winit`, `raw_window_handle`) plus the related terms `EguiHost`,
+`editor_egui_host`, `editor-egui-host`, `rge-editor-egui-host`. Findings
+summarised below; nothing outside the audit roots was searched.
+
+- `editor/rge-editor`: 2 files match (`src/main.rs`, `Cargo.toml`) for
+  `winit` / `tracing` / glTF terms. **No** `egui` / `EguiHost` /
+  `editor-egui-host` direct matches in `editor/rge-editor/**`.
+  `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml` lines 21, 35 declare `rge-editor-shell`
+  + `winit` deps. The binary does NOT directly depend on
+  `rge-editor-egui-host`; it only transitively pulls the host through
+  `rge-editor-shell`.
+- `crates/editor-shell`: 6 files match. Decisive evidence found:
+  - `crates/editor-shell/Cargo.toml:32` — production dep
+    `rge-editor-egui-host = { path = "../editor-egui-host" }` with the
+    in-manifest comment "Phase 9 egui host integration — dispatch B:
+    editor-shell hosts the egui+egui_dock pass in its render loop via
+    the headless `rge-editor-egui-host` library landed in dispatch A.
+    This edge is `editor-shell → editor-egui-host`; the reverse
+    direction is forbidden (would create a cycle and foreclose the
+    planned host direction)."
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:186` — `use
+    rge_editor_egui_host::{EguiHost, InspectorHandoff};`.
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:533` — field declaration
+    `pub(crate) egui_host: Option<EguiHost>,` on the `EditorShell`
+    struct. The module doc at `:97-145` describes dispatch B as
+    already landed: "Adds the `egui_host: Option<EguiHost>` field on
+    `EditorShell`, routes winit events through `EguiHost::on_window_
+    event` BEFORE the branches on `!egui_consumed`… forwards resize
+    updates to the host."
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1482-1483` — production
+    decision function `pub(crate) fn should_fire_face_pick(egui_
+    consumed: bool, over_viewport_tab: bool) -> bool { !egui_consumed
+    || over_viewport_tab }`.
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1544-1545` — production
+    winit→egui input adapter call site:
+    `let egui_consumed = if let (Some(host), Some(window)) = (self.
+    egui_host.as_mut(), self.window.as_ref()) { … host.on_window_event(
+    …) }`.
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1597-1602` — production
+    `WindowEvent::Resized` forward to the host (scale-factor / surface
+    size update).
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:1646-1700` — production
+    `MouseInput` and `KeyboardInput` paths gated on `!egui_consumed`
+    (with the `over_viewport` exception for viewport-tab clicks),
+    proving the bus / picker share an input boundary with the live
+    host.
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:261-298` — `EguiHost::new(
+    …)` is constructed in the resumed / render-init path, taking the
+    real `wgpu::Device` + `Queue` + surface configuration + window
+    scale factor + `ViewportId::ROOT`, then stored on
+    `self.egui_host`.
+  - `crates/editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:366-418` — the cuboid pass
+    is encoded into the same `wgpu::CommandEncoder` as the optional
+    egui pass; `LoadOp::Load` preserves the cuboid pixels and the
+    host paints on top. Code comments make this explicit: "Phase F.2
+    — optional egui pass into the same encoder. `LoadOp::Load`
+    preserves the cuboid pixels; egui paints on top. The host owns
+    its `egui_dock::DockState` + tab bodies".
+- `crates/editor-ui`: 17 files match (egui widget surfaces, dock
+  substrate, menu enums). No `editor-egui-host` / `EguiHost`
+  references — editor-ui has no dep on the host crate (confirmed by
+  `crates/editor-ui/Cargo.toml`, which only depends on `egui` +
+  `egui_dock` as widget substrate).
+- `crates/editor-state`: zero matches. As expected — `editor-state` is
+  coordination state, not UI.
+- `crates/editor-actions`: zero matches. As expected — action
+  substrate, no UI dep.
+
+### Manifest reads (for dependency-edge reasoning only)
+
+- `Cargo.toml` (workspace) — confirmed `crates/editor-egui-host` is a
+  workspace member (line 83). Workspace pins for `egui`, `egui-winit`,
+  `egui-wgpu`, `egui_dock` are all aligned to the `0.34` / `0.19`
+  release train recorded in `change.md`.
+- `editor/rge-editor/Cargo.toml` — production deps: `rge-editor-shell`,
+  `rge-cad-core`, `rge-cad-projection`, `rge-kernel-ecs`,
+  `rge-io-gltf`, `rge-brep-render`, `glam`, `winit`, `tracing`,
+  `tracing-subscriber`, `notify`. **No direct `rge-editor-egui-host`
+  dep**; the binary reaches the host only transitively through
+  editor-shell.
+- `crates/editor-shell/Cargo.toml` — production deps include
+  `rge-editor-egui-host = { path = "../editor-egui-host" }` (line 32),
+  `rge-editor-state`, `rge-editor-actions`, `rge-kernel-ecs`,
+  `rge-input`, `rge-cad-projection`, `rge-gfx`, `rge-brep-render`,
+  `rge-cad-core`, `winit`, `wgpu`, `glam`. The edge `editor-shell →
+  editor-egui-host` is declared with an explicit "cycle prevention"
+  comment.
+- `crates/editor-ui/Cargo.toml` — production deps `serde`, `ron`,
+  `thiserror`, `notify`, `blake3`, `egui`, `egui_dock`, `serde_json`,
+  `rge-kernel-plugin-host`, `rge-editor-state`,
+  `rge-kernel-graph-foundation`. **No `egui-winit`, `egui-wgpu`,
+  `editor-egui-host`, `wgpu`, or `winit` deps** — editor-ui remains
+  pure widget substrate.
+- `crates/editor-state/Cargo.toml` — production deps `rge-cad-core`,
+  `rge-kernel-ecs`, `serde`, `ulid`. No UI / host deps.
+- `crates/editor-actions/Cargo.toml` — production deps
+  `rge-kernel-ecs`, `rge-kernel-audit-ledger`,
+  `rge-kernel-diagnostics`, `serde`, `thiserror`, `tracing`. No UI /
+  host deps.
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/Cargo.toml` — production deps `egui`,
+  `egui-winit`, `egui-wgpu`, `egui_dock` (with `serde` feature),
+  `wgpu`, `winit`, `tracing`, `rge-editor-ui`, `rge-editor-state`. The
+  in-manifest comment records that this crate is "intentionally
+  HEADLESS" and depends neither on `rge-editor-shell` (would cycle)
+  nor on the wgpu-aware editor crates; the comment also names the
+  planned dispatch sequence (A: scaffold with struct + constructor +
+  input adapter + resize hook; B: editor-shell wire-up; C:
+  DockState<TabBody> + Inspector + snapshot handoff).
+
+### Architecture-lint surfaces read (for implications only, never run)
+
+- `tools/architecture-lints/exemptions.toml` — only `failure-class`
+  rollout-debt entries for Phase 1.x. **No `forbidden-dep`,
+  `editor-state-ownership`, or `snapshot-participate` exemptions touch
+  any audited surface.** No exemption masks the editor-shell ↔
+  editor-egui-host edge.
+- `tools/architecture-lints/src/forbidden_dep.rs` — six-rule DAG:
+  - Rule 1 (Tier 1 cannot depend on Tier 2): not implicated; both
+    `editor-shell` and `editor-egui-host` are Tier 2.
+  - Rule 2 (Tier 2 → Tier 3): vacuous; no Tier-3 crates today.
+  - Rule 3 (`rge-cad-core` stands alone): not implicated; cad-core is
+    untouched.
+  - Rule 4 (`rge-editor-ui` cannot depend on physics/audio/input):
+    `editor-ui` continues to depend on neither the host crate nor any
+    forbidden Tier-2 — safe.
+  - Rule 5 (physics → script-host): not implicated.
+  - Rule 6 (renderer → game-domain): not implicated;
+    `editor-egui-host` is `rge-editor-*`-prefixed (matches the
+    "game-domain" prefix list by rule 6's classifier), but its only
+    consumer `editor-shell` is NOT in `RENDERER_CRATES = ["rge-gfx",
+    "rge-gfx-ir", "rge-brep-render"]`. Editor-shell is editor-tier,
+    not renderer-tier.
+- `tools/architecture-lints/src/editor_state_ownership.rs` — Part A
+  forbids defining `Selection` / `Hover` / `ActiveTool` / `ModalState`
+  / `DragDrop` outside `crates/editor-state/`. `editor-egui-host`
+  consumes `rge-editor-state` (the manifest declares the dep), but
+  the recommended path is consumption through coordination IDs /
+  handles, not redefinition. Part B forbids `editor-state` from
+  importing authoritative Tier-2 crates; the prefix/exact lists do
+  NOT include `editor_egui_host`, so an editor-egui-host →
+  editor-state edge does not require any Part B changes.
+- `tools/architecture-lints/src/snapshot_participate.rs` —
+  `STATEFUL_TIER2_CRATES = ["cad-core", "cad-projection", "particles",
+  "physics", "sculpt"]`. `editor-state` is on the explicit removal
+  list ("Editor-state persists across Play/Stop... does NOT
+  participate in `WorldSnapshot`"). An egui host that owns UI /
+  DockState state is similarly **not** a PIE snapshot participant,
+  so no `SnapshotParticipate` impl is owed by the host.
+- `.github/workflows/architecture.yml` — the CI workflow simply runs
+  `cargo run -q -p rge-tool-architecture-lints -- all` on push / PR
+  to main, plus the lint test suite. No editor / host-specific hooks.
+  No workflow edit is required to land or block the existing host
+  wiring.
+
+### Baseline / chronology context
+
+- `plans/BASELINE.md:641-751` ("Live-inspector wiring preflight (Phase
+  9)", dated 2026-05-21) explicitly records the negative finding
+  **"egui host — DOES NOT EXIST"** as of 2026-05-21, names the host
+  absence as the blocker, and recommends "Recommended next dispatch:
+  read-only `egui host integration preflight`" with sub-questions
+  covering placement, input routing, render composition, DockState
+  ownership, snapshot delivery, dep-edge implications, test strategy,
+  and resumed-callback timing. The preflight that ISSUE-106 asks for
+  is exactly that recommended follow-up.
+- However, `editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:97-145` (module doc), the
+  in-manifest comment at `editor-shell/Cargo.toml:26-32`, and the
+  in-manifest comment at `editor-egui-host/Cargo.toml:12-24` all
+  record that **dispatch A (host scaffold) and dispatch B
+  (editor-shell wire-up) have already landed**: editor-shell now owns
+  `egui_host: Option<EguiHost>`, constructs it in the resumed path
+  with the real wgpu device / queue / surface, routes winit
+  `WindowEvent`s through `EguiHost::on_window_event`, gates the
+  picker + bus on `!egui_consumed || over_viewport_tab`, forwards
+  resize / scale-factor changes, and encodes the optional egui pass
+  into the same `wgpu::CommandEncoder` as the cuboid pass. This is
+  the production "code that owns or drives an egui context / frame
+  loop and is wired into the editor host path for real platform
+  input and render composition" that the TASK's halt definition
+  names.
+- `change.md` lines 79-247 record the pre-Phase-9 chronology
+  (workspace egui pin bump 0.27→0.34, egui_dock 0.12→0.19,
+  dock-substrate W10, theme W05). No `editor-egui-host` / `EguiHost`
+  / dispatch A / B rows surfaced in the grep windows inspected; the
+  egui-host landing history may live in a portion of change.md
+  outside the inspected slice, but the in-source / in-manifest doc
+  comments above are themselves the primary chronology evidence the
+  audit needs and the BASELINE.md preflight establishes the prior
+  negative baseline.
+
+### 5-question block heading + Q1-Q5 subheadings check
+
+This report contains the exact line-anchored heading
+`## 5-Question Egui Host Preflight Answer Block` and the exact
+subheadings `### Q1. Host Placement`, `### Q2. Input Routing`,
+`### Q3. Render Composition`, `### Q4. DockState/Snapshot Ownership`,
+`### Q5. Smallest Follow-Up`, in that order. See below.
+
+### Post-write working-tree check
+
+The TASK requires running `git status --short` after writing the EXEC
+packet and asserting that the only Executor-created change is this
+single new file. Because the Markdown is being written by the Executor
+as part of this report, the post-write `git status --short` reading is
+recorded after this report's body is final; it is expected (and
+required) to show exactly one untracked entry under `ai_handoffs/`:
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-106_EXEC_2026-05-23_09-49-07+0300.md` — i.e. the
+new EXEC report itself, plus its sidecar after `new-handoff.ps1
+-Finalize` runs. The pre-existing TASK packet
+(`ISSUE-106_TASK_2026-05-23_09-40-59+0300.md` + sidecar) was already
+untracked at the start of the audit and was not authored by the
+Executor.
+
+---
+
+## 5-Question Egui Host Preflight Answer Block
+
+> **HALT — preflight premise invalidated.** The TASK packet enumerates a
+> halt condition: "A production egui host already exists. For this task,
+> treat a production host as code that owns or drives an egui context /
+> frame loop and is wired into the editor host path for real platform
+> input and render composition, not merely a manifest entry, test
+> fixture, stub module, or inactive experiment." The read-only audit
+> found such a production host already integrated. Per protocol, Q1–Q4
+> are answered with the **descriptive design rationale visible from the
+> audit roots only** (so the human arbiter has the preflight content
+> the planner requested), but **none of these answers constitutes
+> Executor consent to dispatch a substrate change**; Q5 is
+> `NEEDS_HUMAN`. The decisions Q1–Q4 describe have already been chosen
+> by the existing wiring, not by this preflight — the human arbiter
+> must reconcile whether ISSUE-106 should be retired as "already
+> landed", re-scoped as a verification preflight of the existing
+> wiring, or routed elsewhere.
+
+### Q1. Host Placement
+
+**Three placement options compared (descriptive, not prescriptive):**
+
+| Option | Shape | Dep-edge implications | Architecture-lint implications |
+|---|---|---|---|
+| **A — host inside `crates/editor-shell`** | egui Context + egui_winit::State + egui_wgpu::Renderer all owned directly on `EditorShell` fields; the lifecycle module both constructs them and routes input / render | adds `egui`, `egui-winit`, `egui-wgpu`, `egui_dock` direct deps to `editor-shell`; no new workspace crate | rule 4 (`editor-ui` cannot depend on physics/audio/input) unaffected; rule 6 unaffected (editor-shell is editor-tier, not renderer-tier); editor-state-ownership Part B unaffected (host is in editor-shell, not editor-state); however, this option blocks the planned headless-host test surface — editor-shell already owns winit + wgpu and is not headlessly compileable without `test-harness` feature gating |
+| **B — new `crates/editor-egui-host` crate between editor-shell and editor-ui** | host owns Context + egui_winit::State + egui_wgpu::Renderer + DockState; **headless** crate; editor-shell holds an `Option<EguiHost>` field; editor-shell calls `host.on_window_event` + `host.render_into` per frame; the host depends on editor-ui (for the widgets) + editor-state (for snapshot coordination types) but **NOT** on editor-shell (would cycle) | new edges: `editor-shell → editor-egui-host`, `editor-egui-host → editor-ui`, `editor-egui-host → editor-state`, plus external `egui` / `egui-winit` / `egui-wgpu` / `egui_dock` / `wgpu` / `winit` | rule 6 not triggered (host is not in `RENDERER_CRATES`; only `rge-gfx` / `rge-gfx-ir` / `rge-brep-render` are renderer-tier; host crate is `rge-editor-egui-host` — `editor-*` prefix is "game-domain" under rule 6's classifier, but the `editor-shell` consumer is editor-tier, not renderer); rule 4 not triggered (host crate is not `rge-editor-ui`); editor-state-ownership Part B unaffected (only `editor-state` itself is gated by Part B); a future host → editor-shell back-edge would create a cycle and is forbidden |
+| **C — host in the `editor/rge-editor` binary only** | Context + State + Renderer constructed in `main.rs` and threaded into editor-shell via an opaque callback | binary becomes the integration point; editor-shell stays "pure lifecycle"; future non-binary integrators (golden harnesses, automation scripts) cannot reuse the host | rule 6 / 4 / Part B all unaffected because the binary is not a lint-gated crate; **but** all snapshot / DockState / input plumbing leaks into `main.rs`, defeating the editor-shell skeleton's purpose and making test parity impossible |
+
+**Audit observation (not a recommendation):** The repository **already
+chose Option B**. `crates/editor-egui-host` exists as a workspace
+member; `editor-shell` declares `rge-editor-egui-host` as a production
+dep (line 32 of `editor-shell/Cargo.toml`) with the explicit
+cycle-prevention comment; the host crate declares `rge-editor-ui` +
+`rge-editor-state` deps and intentionally does NOT depend on
+`rge-editor-shell`; and the editor binary reaches the host
+transitively through editor-shell, not directly. None of the
+forbidden-dep rules that bear on the new edges fires. **This Q1
+answer therefore describes a decision that has already been taken —
+it is not the Executor proposing Option B over A / C.**
+
+### Q2. Input Routing
+
+**Smallest safe input-routing shape (descriptive only — the existing
+wiring already implements this shape):**
+
+- **Platform-input layer** = `editor-shell`. `editor-shell` owns the
+  winit `ApplicationHandler` and receives raw `WindowEvent`s in
+  `lifecycle::window_event`. Per audit, this is the only crate in the
+  audit roots that consumes raw winit events; the host crate must NOT
+  re-implement `ApplicationHandler` (would cycle into editor-shell).
+- **Egui-consumption decision layer** = the host crate, exposed as a
+  single `on_window_event(&mut self, window, event) -> EventResponse`
+  call. The host wraps `egui_winit::State::on_window_event`, which is
+  the canonical adapter that returns `{ consumed: bool, repaint: bool
+  }` for a given `WindowEvent`. Per audit, `editor-shell/src/
+  lifecycle/mod.rs:1544-1545` already calls this adapter as
+  `host.on_window_event(window, &event)` BEFORE any picker / bus
+  branch and stores the resulting `consumed` flag.
+- **Pre-existing input boundaries that MUST remain bounded:**
+  - The CommandBus + UndoStack pipeline in `editor-actions` continues
+    to receive only `EditorKeyCommand` translations of winit
+    `KeyboardInput` events — gated on `!egui_consumed` so a key typed
+    into an egui text field is not double-applied as a bus command.
+    Audit citation: `lifecycle/mod.rs:1688-1700`.
+  - The face-picker in `editor-shell::pick_path` continues to receive
+    only `MouseInput` events that satisfy
+    `should_fire_face_pick(egui_consumed, over_viewport_tab) =
+    !egui_consumed || over_viewport_tab`. The exception (egui-consumed
+    but pointer over the viewport tab body) is documented at
+    `lifecycle/mod.rs:1457-1483`; it is load-bearing because the
+    egui_dock area covers the whole window, so without it every
+    viewport click would be eaten by egui.
+  - The `EditorKeyCommand` translation continues to live in
+    `crates/input::translate_keyboard`; editor-shell owns the only
+    `rge-input` dep (rule 4 still forbids editor-ui from doing so).
+    Adding an editor-egui-host → input edge is not required and is
+    not present in the audit; the host does not need keyboard
+    translation.
+- **`ModifiersChanged` + `CursorMoved` must reach both the host and
+  the editor's own modifier-state machine** (audit citation:
+  `lifecycle/mod.rs:1540` — "ModifiersChanged + CursorMoved are
+  observed by BOTH egui and..."). This is not a single-consumer event;
+  the host gets a copy via `on_window_event` and the picker / bus keep
+  their own copy of the current modifier mask.
+- **Resize + scale-factor changes are forwarded to the host
+  explicitly, not via `on_window_event`** (audit citation:
+  `lifecycle/mod.rs:1597-1602`). The host needs the new logical size +
+  dpi to reconfigure its `egui_wgpu::Renderer` viewport state and its
+  `egui::Context::set_pixels_per_point`. This is a separate API
+  surface (`host.on_resized` / `host.on_scale_factor_changed`)
+  deliberately distinct from `on_window_event` because resize is also
+  consumed by the wgpu surface reconfigure path in
+  `editor-shell::render_path`.
+
+### Q3. Render Composition
+
+**Design-level render composition (descriptive — the existing wiring
+already implements this composition):**
+
+- **Egui frame lifecycle**:
+  1. `egui::Context::begin_frame` (host) at the start of the egui
+     pass, consuming the `RawInput` accumulated by `egui-winit::State`
+     since the previous frame.
+  2. User-supplied closure runs all UI code against `&mut egui::Ui`
+     (`DockArea::show`, widget calls, etc.).
+  3. `egui::Context::end_frame` returns a `FullOutput` containing the
+     `ShapeVec` + texture-delta + platform-output.
+  4. `egui_wgpu::Renderer::update_buffers` uploads vertex / index /
+     uniform buffers for the frame.
+  5. `egui_wgpu::Renderer::render` records a render pass that paints
+     the ShapeVec into the target view.
+- **Draw-data / texture handling**: textures are owned by
+  `egui_wgpu::Renderer` (which maintains a `HashMap<TextureId,
+  wgpu::Texture>` and applies `TexturesDelta::set` / `::free` each
+  frame). The host crate keeps this entirely internal; the editor
+  never touches an egui texture directly.
+- **Relationship to the existing render path**: the existing main
+  pass in `editor-shell::render_path` clears the surface, sets the
+  lit-mesh pipeline, encodes one cuboid `draw_indexed`, optionally
+  encodes a highlight overlay, and closes the pass. The egui pass is
+  recorded **into the same `wgpu::CommandEncoder` immediately after
+  the highlight overlay close and before `queue().submit()`**, using
+  `LoadOp::Load` so the cuboid + highlight pixels are preserved and
+  egui paints on top. This avoids a second `submit()` and keeps the
+  frame as a single GPU command stream. Audit citation:
+  `editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:366-418` and the in-source comment
+  at `:843-878` noting the visual-test harness deliberately excludes
+  the egui pass.
+- **Surfaces that MUST remain outside any follow-up dispatch**:
+  - The cuboid + highlight passes (no edits to lit-mesh pipeline,
+    camera bind groups, material bind groups).
+  - The visual-test harness in
+    `editor-shell/src/visual_test_harness.rs` — it deliberately
+    renders egui-free for byte-stable pixel goldens, so its
+    `encode_main_pass` path must not pick up an unconditional egui
+    pass.
+  - `editor-shell::render_input::RenderHandoff` (the existing
+    sim → render boundary). The egui pass consumes UI-side state, not
+    sim-side state; it does not need a RenderHandoff slot.
+  - Any wgpu surface acquisition / configuration — those stay in
+    `editor-shell::render_path`. The host receives a `wgpu::
+    TextureView` + `wgpu::CommandEncoder` from the caller; it never
+    owns the surface.
+  - Inspector-tab body wiring (per planner notes; that is a
+    deliberately separate later dispatch).
+
+### Q4. DockState/Snapshot Ownership
+
+**Ownership rules (descriptive — already realised by the existing
+wiring):**
+
+- **`DockState<TabBody>`** is owned by the host crate
+  (`crates/editor-egui-host`), not by `editor-shell` and not by
+  `editor-ui`. Rationale:
+  - `editor-ui` defines `TabBody` shapes + widget render functions
+    but must remain headless (no wgpu, no winit, no egui Context);
+    putting `DockState` on an editor-ui type would force editor-ui
+    to take a live host borrow per frame.
+  - `editor-shell` is the platform-input + render-path skeleton and
+    must not couple to egui_dock state directly (would cycle the
+    deps: editor-shell already owns the winit `ApplicationHandler`
+    and would become both the host driver and the UI authority
+    simultaneously).
+  - The host crate sits between them, owning `DockState<TabBody>`
+    and exposing `dock_area_show(&mut self, ui, …)` plus a
+    snapshot-handoff accessor. Audit citation:
+    `editor-shell/src/render_path.rs:405` ("the host owns its
+    `egui_dock::DockState` + tab bodies").
+- **Editor snapshots** (the `rge_editor_state::InspectorSnapshot`
+  shape and any future `WorldSnapshot` surface in the host) are
+  **produced** by editor-shell (`EditorShell::inspector_snapshot()`
+  — per the prior preflight in BASELINE.md) and **consumed**
+  read-only by the host. The authoritative `WorldSnapshot` continues
+  to live in `kernel/ecs::Snapshot`; the inspector projection lives
+  in `rge_editor_state::InspectorSnapshot` (a flat `Copy` struct,
+  per BASELINE.md). Audit citation:
+  `editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:538-552` (the host stores a
+  copy of the projection in an `InspectorTabBody` via a
+  once-per-frame handoff; the live world is never moved into the
+  host).
+- **What MUST NOT move into the host:**
+  - Authoritative `World` (kernel/ecs) — stays on editor-shell.
+  - The CommandBus + UndoStack — stay in `editor-actions`; the host
+    has no direct dep on `editor-actions`.
+  - `Selection` / `Hover` / `ActiveTool` / `ModalState` / `DragDrop`
+    — stay in `editor-state`; Part A of editor-state-ownership lint
+    enforces this statically and would fire if the host tried to
+    redefine them.
+  - Project-level dirty / save state — stays in editor-shell /
+    editor-actions.
+- **What the host owns (and only the host):**
+  - `egui::Context` + `egui_winit::State` + `egui_wgpu::Renderer`.
+  - `DockState<TabBody>` and the `TabBody` enum (which holds widget
+    state, not editor authority).
+  - The per-frame `RawInput` accumulation and `FullOutput`
+    consumption.
+  - The texture-delta + screen-rect bookkeeping owned by
+    `egui_wgpu::Renderer`.
+
+### Q5. Smallest Follow-Up
+
+**`NEEDS_HUMAN`.**
+
+The audit evidence shows that the egui host substrate the TASK packet
+was written to design (Phase 9 dispatch A = scaffold + Phase 9
+dispatch B = editor-shell wire-up) **has already landed in the
+workspace** under the ISSUE-106 dispatch branch
+(`ai-dispatch/ISSUE-106`, current `git log` HEAD `140e4e7`). The
+TASK's halt condition explicitly names this case: "If a production
+egui host already exists, the Executor halts and writes an
+`EXECUTION_REPORT` with `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`".
+
+The Executor cannot safely nominate a "smallest follow-up dispatch"
+without human judgment on **which of the following the human arbiter
+intends**, because each leads to a meaningfully different next
+dispatch and the Executor lacks the authority to pick between them:
+
+1. **Retire ISSUE-106.** If dispatches A + B have landed (the
+   in-manifest and in-source doc comments at
+   `editor-shell/Cargo.toml:26-32`,
+   `editor-egui-host/Cargo.toml:12-24`, and
+   `editor-shell/src/lifecycle/mod.rs:97-145` all assert this), then
+   the read-only Phase-9 host integration preflight has been
+   superseded by the actual landed code and should be closed as
+   `ABANDONED` or `CLOSED` without a follow-up. The next bounded
+   dispatch would then be dispatch C (DockState<TabBody> + Inspector
+   + snapshot handoff, per the host crate's manifest comment) — but
+   that is the very "inspector-tab wiring" dispatch the TASK's "do
+   not combine substrate + tab wiring" guard rail asks the Executor
+   NOT to roll into the same dispatch as the substrate work.
+2. **Re-scope ISSUE-106 as a verification preflight of the landed
+   wiring.** A read-only dispatch that audits the existing host
+   wiring for compliance with the BASELINE.md questions, confirms
+   the lints do not regress, and records the realised answers to
+   Q1–Q4 as ADR / doc updates (changing `plans/BASELINE.md` and
+   `change.md`, which the present TASK forbids). This is a
+   different TASK shape — its MAY-edit list would include the
+   baseline + change.md, and its scope would be pure documentation,
+   not design.
+3. **Treat the landed wiring as out-of-scope and recommend
+   recision.** If dispatches A + B were landed by an earlier
+   dispatch that the human did not authorise, the recovery action
+   is a `git` investigation + a possible revert dispatch —
+   strictly a human judgement call.
+4. **Tab-wiring follow-up.** If the human is content with the
+   substrate as landed and wants the next forward step, the
+   smallest forward dispatch is dispatch C (DockState<TabBody> +
+   Inspector + snapshot handoff). The TASK's planner notes
+   explicitly call out that "inspector-tab wiring is explicitly a
+   later dispatch" — meaning it is forward work, not bundled with
+   substrate, which lines up with this option being a clean bounded
+   follow-up if and only if the substrate is accepted as-is.
+
+**Smallest decision needed from a human arbiter (one sentence):**
+*Confirm whether the existing `crates/editor-egui-host` +
+editor-shell host wiring is the intended landed state of ISSUE-106 —
+if yes, retire the preflight and route the next forward dispatch
+(dispatch C: inspector-tab + snapshot handoff); if no, route a
+recision / revert dispatch before any further forward work.*
+

+
+## Open Questions for Reviewer
+
+1. **Is `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN` the correct verdict, or should this be

+## Goal
+
+Audit GitHub issue #106, "Audit egui host integration shape", as a read-only design preflight before any implementation dispatch. The Executor must inspect the existing editor host, shell, UI, state, actions, relevant manifests, relevant architecture-lint surfaces, `plans/BASELINE.md`, and `change.md`, then produce one `EXECUTION_REPORT` answering the required five egui host preflight questions without editing production code, tests, docs, manifests, workflows, scripts, or existing packets.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- None. This is an audit-only dispatch.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `editor/rge-editor/**`
+- `crates/editor-shell/**`
+- `crates/editor-ui/**`
+- `crates/editor-state/**`
+- `crates/editor-actions/**`
+- `crates/editor-egui-host/**`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- Any `Cargo.toml`, workspace manifest, dependency declaration, feature flag, or Cargo metadata file.
+- `tools/architecture-lints/**`
+- `.github/workflows/**`
+- `plans/BASELINE.md`
+- `change.md`
+- Any source file, test file, documentation file, status file, schema, script, workflow, lint configuration, `.gitignore`, generated artifact, persisted log, or repository metadata file.
+- Any existing handoff packet, including this TASK packet.
+- Any handoff packet other than the single required `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-106_EXEC_*.md` execution report.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-106_EXEC_*.md`.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New source files, tests, docs, fixtures, generated artifacts, persisted logs, screenshots, Cargo entries, dependency files, crates, architecture lints, schemas, scripts, workflow files, TASK packets, REVIEW packets, CORRECT packets, or CLOSEOUT packets.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet under `ai_handoffs/` that documents the read-only audit evidence, inspected paths, search terms, placement comparison, and final recommendation or halt reason.
+- The `EXECUTION_REPORT` must contain this exact answer-block heading and these exact Q1-Q5 subheadings, in this order:
+
+```markdown
+## 5-Question Egui Host Preflight Answer Block
+
+### Q1. Host Placement
+
+### Q2. Input Routing
+
+### Q3. Render Composition
+
+### Q4. DockState/Snapshot Ownership
+
+### Q5. Smallest Follow-Up
+```
+
+- Q1 must compare host placement in `crates/editor-shell`, a new or existing `crates/editor-egui-host` crate shape, and the `editor/rge-editor` binary, including dependency-edge and architecture-lint implications.
+- Q2 must describe the smallest safe input-routing shape for egui host integration, including which layer should receive platform input, which layer should decide egui consumption, and how the existing editor input/action surfaces must remain bounded.
+- Q3 must describe render composition at design level only: egui frame lifecycle, draw-data or texture handling, relationship to the existing render path, and surfaces that must remain outside the follow-up dispatch.
+- Q4 must identify who should own `DockState` and editor snapshots, and must explain how the design avoids moving editor authority or snapshot ownership into an egui host.
+- Q5 must name exactly one smallest follow-up dispatch with allowed files, must-not-touch surfaces, verification gates, and halt conditions. If no bounded follow-up is safe from the audit evidence, Q5 must say `NEEDS_HUMAN` and explain the smallest decision needed from a human.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- No existing file is modified by the Executor.
+- Exactly one new `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-106_EXEC_*.md` report is produced.
+- The report explicitly states this was a read-only egui host design preflight and that no local cargo commands, tests, formatters, architecture lints, or `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` were run.
+- The report documents read-only inspection of all mandatory audit roots: `editor/rge-editor/**`, `crates/editor-shell/**`, `crates/editor-ui/**`, `crates/editor-state/**`, and `crates/editor-actions/**`.
+- The report documents inspection of `plans/BASELINE.md` and `change.md`.
+- The report documents inspection of the relevant manifests and architecture-lint surfaces needed for dependency-edge reasoning, limited to the files listed in `RELATED_FILES`.
+- The report contains the exact `## 5-Question Egui Host Preflight Answer Block` heading and the exact Q1-Q5 subheadings listed in `Deliverables`.
+- Q1 compares `crates/editor-shell`, `crates/editor-egui-host`, and `editor/rge-editor` binary placement as host options and names dependency-edge plus architecture-lint consequences for each.
+- Q2 covers input routing and event-consumption boundaries without proposing implementation edits.
+- Q3 covers render composition boundaries without proposing inspector-tab wiring.
+- Q4 covers `DockState` and snapshot ownership and names the crate or layer that should retain ownership.
+- Q5 names one smallest follow-up dispatch or `NEEDS_HUMAN`; it must not combine egui host substrate and inspector-tab wiring into one follow-up.

+- If all audit questions are answered without a halt condition, the `EXECUTION_REPORT` footer contains exactly one line-anchored `HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE` marker and routes `NEXT_ROLE: REVIEWER_AI`.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This dispatch is read-only design preflight. It must not implement egui host substrate, wire inspector tabs, create a crate, change dependencies, change manifests, or change architecture lints.
+- Do not run local cargo commands, tests, formatters, architecture lints, or `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`.
+- Do not run `cargo check`, `cargo test`, `cargo fmt`, `cargo clippy`, `cargo run -p rge-tool-architecture-lints`, or any equivalent command.

The only allowed `crates/editor-egui-host` path is `crates/editor-egui-host/Cargo.toml` for manifest/dependency context. If source inspection there is required to answer whether a production egui host already exists, halt with `NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- Do not commit, push, merge, open a PR, or add GitHub issue comments.

+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each of these in their `EXECUTION_REPORT`. These gates are read-only only:
+
+- Run `git status --short --untracked-files=no` before the audit. Document any pre-existing tracked changes. If tracked changes inside the audit surfaces make read-only conclusions unreliable, halt with `NEEDS_HUMAN`; do not clean or revert them.

Use `rg` if available; otherwise use an equivalent PowerShell recursive text search. At minimum search for `egui`, `egui_winit`, `egui_wgpu`, `Egui`, `DockState`, `snapshot`, `Inspector`, `RenderInput`, `render_frame`, `winit`, and `raw_window_handle`.
+- Read the relevant manifests listed in `RELATED_FILES`, including `crates/editor-egui-host/Cargo.toml` if present, for dependency-edge reasoning only.

+- Read `plans/BASELINE.md` and `change.md` for baseline and chronology context.

+- Run `git status --short` after writing the EXEC packet. Expected result: no Executor-created changes outside the single new `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-106_EXEC_*.md` report, aside from any pre-existing tracked changes documented before the audit.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without editing existing files and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- A production egui host already exists. For this task, treat a production host as code that owns or drives an egui context/frame loop and is wired into the editor host path for real platform input and render composition, not merely a manifest entry, test fixture, stub module, or inactive experiment.
+- Determining whether a production egui host exists requires reading outside the allowed audit roots and relevant manifest/lint files.
+- Answering Q1-Q5 requires editing source, tests, docs, `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, workflows, scripts, lints, `plans/BASELINE.md`, `change.md`, existing packets, or any other non-EXEC file.
+- The audit requires inspecting `crates/editor-egui-host/src/**`, `crates/editor-egui-host/tests/**`, or any other out-of-scope implementation directory.
+- A required audit root is missing or unreadable.
+- The only plausible smallest follow-up would combine egui host substrate and inspector-tab wiring in one dispatch.
+- The smallest follow-up cannot be bounded with explicit allowed files, must-not-touch surfaces, verification gates, and halt conditions.
+- The Executor accidentally runs a prohibited cargo command, test, formatter, architecture lint, or `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`; if this happens, stop immediately and report `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`.
+- The final working tree contains Executor-created changes outside the one new `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-106_EXEC_*.md` report.
+
+If a halt condition fires, the Executor must still write the single EXEC report if possible. The report should include the required 5-question block; unanswered questions may say `HALTED:` with the concrete reason, and Q5 must say `NEEDS_HUMAN`. Use header `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN`, and `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER` when human arbitration is needed. Use `STATUS: BLOCKED`, footer `HANDOFF_STATUS: BLOCKED`, and `NEXT_ROLE: PLANNER_AI` only for a narrow missing-context blocker that the Planner can resolve without human product or architecture judgment.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The goal is to decide the host integration shape before any source edit. Keep the preflight focused on where egui belongs, how input crosses the boundary, how egui composition relates to the existing renderer, and where `DockState` and snapshots stay authoritative. The next dispatch, if one is safe, should be the smallest egui host substrate step only; inspector-tab wiring is explicitly a later dispatch.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is expected from this TASK. The only new packet produced by execution should be the `ISSUE-106_EXEC_*.md` report.
+
